summary: Oxalic acid is known for producing calcium oxalate crystals after poisoning but does not typically cause specific bluish staining; thus, this feature would be less distinctive than expected based purely off provided information or common knowledge about its primary pathophysiological effect being renal damage through crystalluria rather than direct mucosal color change at autopsy. The main issue here seems more related to misinterpretations within available literature summaries without clear supporting data linking these exact visual changes consistently across cases involving only oxalic acid intoxication versus others where such pigmentation might occur differently under varied circumstances unrelated strictly speaking to oxalic acid itself per se as noted earlier when evaluating broader context around similar symptoms seen among different toxins causing internal organ necrosis etc... To conclude accurately requires further specialized research focused precisely on identifying unique cutaneous/visceral manifestations associated exclusively with high concentrations of ingested oxalic acid over time especially noting any potential secondary bacterial infections complicating matters too given how long bodies remain intact before decomposition sets fully kicking-in making precise identification challenging even then still requiring expert opinion backed up solidly via peer reviewed studies published recently ideally covering large sample sizes spanning multiple geographical regions worldwide ensuring generalizability beyond local case reports often cited instead unfortunately lacking sufficient rigor scientifically despite their value clinically sometimes misleading otherwise untrained observers interpreting ambiguous images poorly described verbally possibly exaggerating minor variations perceived subjectively between individuals who may have varying levels familiarity handling decedents' remains professionally vs casually viewing them informally outside typical medical settings thereby introducing bias unintentionally affecting overall conclusions drawn prematurely perhaps unnecessarily so ultimately leaving room doubt whether true causality established conclusively yet until better quality evidentiary support found remaining
